analysis_description: Round-3 tapes days 0–2: TTE from round3description (8/7/6 d open per csv day) plus intraday winding via plot_iv_smile_round3.t_years_effective (same as voucher_work). Per-timestamp BS implied vol from mid, vega from analytic formula; neighbor metric = max−min of (wall_mid−BS_theo) across strikes within 350 of extract mid. Corr(|ΔS|, neighbor gap) ≈ 0 near zero (no strong linear link); ATM IV medians ~0.24–0.25.
used_to_decide: Grounded pause/resume in observables (extract step vs slow EMA in code; neighbor residual spread as alignment gate). Motivated widening OTM after jumps via pause, not a separate product thesis.

analysis_description: Diagnostic on tape (day 0 sample): Frankfurt get_iv_scalping_orders with default cal produced zero order tuples on 4990/4990 ticks where neighbor compression held — switch_mean EMA of |ctd−mean| stayed below IV_SCALPING_THR 0.7. Informed later iterations to stop relying on that gate alone.
used_to_decide: Explained zero VEV PnL in v0–v2 despite neighbor gate; led to v4 signal on current_theo_diff vs its EMA instead.

analysis_description: Naive mean-reversion on raw (wall_mid−theo) minus its EMA backtested sharply negative (−21k); falsified that specific implementation while keeping jump+neighbor structure.
used_to_decide: Abandoned raw diff-EMA; retained thesis filters for v4.

analysis_description: Jump-regime IV/Greek check on all ROUND_3 tapes (days 0–2): classify jump timestamps by |Δextract mid| above day-wise 95th percentile. ATM IV and ATM vega are almost unchanged between jump and non-jump regimes (jump_iv_mean 0.2440 vs nonjump 0.2438; jump_vega_mean 271.27 vs nonjump 271.94). This supports making the post-jump pause/neighbor gate mostly microstructure-driven, with only a light vega adjustment.
used_to_decide: Implemented v6 as a smaller, regime-aware tweak of v1: shorter pause and vega-adjusted neighbor residual cap, without changing product thesis.

analysis_description: No new offline tape script: v7 merges v1 (jump pause + hydrogel/extract on pause) with v4 VEV logic (current_theo_diff − mean_theo_diff scalp, bb−1/ba+1). Backtest total 15,353 vs v1 24,318 — VEV adds real PnL but day-2 voucher drawdowns outweighed the hydrogel carry. Conclusion: combining both alpha paths needs separate tuning (e.g. drop extract taker on pause, or v4 params only) before it beats pure-hydrogel v1 on this metric.
used_to_decide: Record hybrid attempt; best overall remains v1/v2; next iteration should not blindly stack theo_scalp on top of v1 without ablating extract fade or matching v4 thresholds first.

analysis_description: Ablate-only comparison from backtests: v7 day-0 had +238 on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT (light fade on pause) alongside VEV. v8 removes those extract orders. Result: day-0 total 8,294 vs v7 8,542; day-2 total −2,698 vs v7 −4,945 (2.2k better); combined +1,989 vs v7. Still below v1/v2 (24,318) because VEV PnL on this signal does not cover lost Frankfurt-zero hydrogel-only path. No new Greek tape script — v8 reuses the same BS smile/vega path as v4 in compute_option_indicators.
used_to_decide: Dropping extract taker on pause is a real improvement for the theo-hybrid; next grid could try v4-only VEV (no hydrogel) vs v1, or add spread filter on resumption to cut day-2 wing losses.

analysis_description: Off-line: analyze_atm_vev_spread.py — for each row timestamp, nearest-ATM VEV to extract mid, width = best_ask_1−best_bid_1, optional ATM vega via get_option_values and t_years_effective. Pooled 30k rows: width p50=2, p90=p95=3 ticks; per-day p95 also 3. Tight two–three tick top-of-book is universal, so a max-width cap would rarely gate (unlike the neighbor-theo misalignment). v9 backtest: tighter neighbor 6.5 + thr 0.45 + clip 18 underperformed v8 (16,398 vs 17,342) — more selectivity did not help day-2 VEV; keep v8/v4-threshold as stronger hybrid.
used_to_decide: Argued against bid-ask width as the main resume filter; next iteration should either stay at v8 params or test orthogonal filters (e.g. per-day only, or skip VEV in last session segment) rather than neighbor/threshold grids that mirror v5’s miss.

analysis_description: New tape analysis (analysis_time_bucket_dev_vega.json): nearest-ATM |wall_mid−BS_theo| and ATM vega by session quartile, using T=t_years_effective and smile coeffs via get_option_values. Q4 has the worst tails and lowest vega (abs_dev_p90 4.01 vs ~3.18–3.50 in Q1–Q3; vega_p50 263.9 vs 270–278; corr(q,abs_dev)=+0.153, corr(q,vega)=-0.329). This supports reducing new VEV risk late-session after jump-pause resumes.
used_to_decide: Implemented v10 late-session entry cutoff (q>0.75 no new VEV entries, exits still allowed). Backtest improved over v8 (18,791 vs 17,342), especially day-2 (-2,379 vs -2,698), while keeping the same jump-gap-filter thesis.

analysis_description: No new off-line numbers (iteration 10 analysis_time_bucket_dev_vega.json still the basis). v11 is a one-parameter grid on v10: earlier VEV entry cutoff 0.70 instead of 0.75. Backtest 17,506 total vs v10 18,791 — cutting entries earlier in Q3 also removed profitable early-Q4 theo_scalp, so 0.75 is preferred. Day-2 identical to v10 (-2,379), day-0/1 down.
used_to_decide: Keep v10 cutoff at 0.75 for the hybrid; next sweeps if any should nudge 0.78–0.80 or add day-2–only stricter cap rather than 0.70.

analysis_description: Family12 pre-analysis (analysis_family12_refit_lag.json): compare fixed refit cadence (40 ticks) vs jump-aware immediate refit for cubic-spline(logK/S) smile from tape IVs. Post-jump MAE improves slightly (0.001542 -> 0.001531), though global MAE worsens. Implemented jump-aware refit in trader_v12 and saved run artifacts (iter12_backtest.log + iter12_fill_summary.json). Backtest PnL is exactly identical to v10 (18,791), implying this variation is behaviorally near-equivalent under current quoting path (signal still dominated by coeff-based indicator branch).
used_to_decide: This is not literally pre-existing in this branch, but in practice it behaves like v10 at execution level; further family12 impact likely requires routing entry/exit signal itself through spline-refit deltas (not just auxiliary theo path).

analysis_description: Duplicate-check vs origin/r3v_smile_cubic_spline_logk_12: parent explored full spline book (v0–v13) with global/LOO IV smile, vega-spread edge scaling, multi-level takes, delta headroom grids; best ~68.9k. This child’s prior trader_v12 was HIGH duplicate risk: it layered a jump refit on the wrong strategy stack and matched v10 PnL (18,791). trader_v13 realigns to parent’s actual family-12 stack (v1 take/MM) but changes assumptions: intraday T from t_years_effective, LOO IV at each strike, and sticky refit policy. Backtest 47,709 (worse fills) — below parent best but NOT a duplicate execution path.
used_to_decide: Record EXACT_DUPLICATE_CHECK in assistant output; keep v13 as the non-duplicate family-12 pivot on this branch.

parent_explored: Cubic spline IV(log K) from per-strike BS IV vs extract; robust global IV outlier drop (ROBUST_IV_RANGE 0.35).
v1: live extract wall mid S for spline/option fair; smoothed _fex for extract/hydrogel cash quotes.
Edge/MM take+make ladder on all strikes; vega×spread edge scaling (v5–v8).
v9+: leave-one-out spline IV per strike fair; v10–v13 delta-headroom inventory taper grids.
Analysis: LOO vs in-sample fair gaps, strike-edge+Greek CSVs, iv_spline_summary, etc.
my_variant_delta: Uses parent v1 order engine + v9 LOO fair IV, but T from t_years_effective (intraday DTE wind-down) vs parent constant (8−d)/365.25.
Jump/cadence sticky refit of robust (*x,y*) nodes; between refits fair uses cached smile at new (S,T) — not in parent history.
No vega-spread scaling / delta taper from parent v5–v11 (iteration-1 minimal port).
exact_duplicate_check.rationale: Prior local trader_v12 duplicated jump-gap v10; v13 is a different product/economy stack and code path from parent v11, though shares the abstract 'cubic spline in logK' name.

analysis_description: Underlying propagation by strike (analysis_underlying_propagation_by_strike*.csv/json): regress dVEV_mid on dExtract_mid per strike/day and compare spread pre/post 95th-percentile extract shocks. Betas: high around 4000-5100 (~0.58-0.75), lower 5300-5400 (~0.13-0.27), near-zero 6000/6500. Shock spread deltas are small except tails; actionable signal is response beta heterogeneity. Kept substantive Greek thread via same BS/IV/LOO smile (v13 family) and used strike response buckets to modulate quoting/taking intensity in v14.
used_to_decide: Very-low-beta strikes (6000/6500/5500) were mostly noise under worse fills; v14 suppresses or shrinks those while preserving high-beta/core strikes. Result 46,996 < v13 47,709, so keep v13 lead and treat beta-bucket attenuation as over-pruning for this tape.

analysis_description: Up/down *residual* asymmetry on *large* |dS| (pooled R3, consecutive timestamp pairs): pooled OLS betas dV/dS are near-symmetric by sign, but for |dS| at or above the 80th percentile (~1.5 price units) the mean residual (dV - bpool*dS) is negative on up moves and positive on down for strikes 4000-5500 (e.g. VEV_5000 gap ~0.11). Suggests voucher mids lag a fixed-β linear model after big extract moves. IV/Greek path unchanged from v13 (BS from LOO fair IV, intraday T).
used_to_decide: parent v15 from v13 (not v14). Apply per-strike λ = mean_resid_up - mean_resid_down from the large-move block: shift BS fair by -sign(dS_ex)*w*λ with w cap 0.45 and |dS| scale to jump threshold 3, gate |dS|>=0.4. Interprets 'pause/widen' regime as *signed* dislocation (buy after up when fair is nudged down vs LSMILE). Day-2 PnL improves vs v14 (11,898 vs 12,084) while day-0 matches v14’s lift on 8,970; three-day total 47,762 > v14 46,996 and v13 47,709. Ops: prosperity4bt defaults to opening the web visualizer; use --no-vis for non-interactive backtests (otherwise process can block after day 1).

analysis_description: Lead/lag + spread asymmetry around signed extract shock95 events (analysis_underlying_leadlag_spread_asym.json): estimate same-tick beta and next-tick beta by strike and sign. Down-shock next-tick follow-through is relatively stronger at 5100/5200/5400 (and mildly 4000), while many other strikes have flat or opposite asymmetry. IV/Greek framework unchanged: LOO cubic-spline IV + BS fair with intraday T.
used_to_decide: Test sign-asymmetric fair tilt multiplier by strike (reduce/reverse down-shock tilt where down follow-through is stronger). This reduced day-0 by ~172 and total to 47,590 (<47,762 v15), so keep v15 as incumbent and treat this as a falsified extension.

analysis_description: Cross-strike residual range ptp = max_k (wall_mid - LOO BS fair) - min_k (same) on all 30k R3 timestamps (days 0-2), same IV/spline pipeline as family-12. Median ptp is ~flat for |dS|>=3 vs not (\u22486.47), but tail is worse on jump ticks: p90_jump \u2248138 vs p90_nojump \u2248110 (analysis_neighbor_residual_ptp_jump.json). Method: BS IV from mid, robust spline nodes, LOO fair per strike, analytic vega/delta thread unchanged in trader.
used_to_decide: Re-embed core thesis 'jump then widen; resume when neighbor residuals compress' on top of v15: after |dS|>=3, pause *new* VEV orders (no flatten), resume when ptp <= 0.88 * ptp at jump or 100 ticks. Backtest 63,106 vs v15 47,762 (largest lift day 2: 11,898 -> 29,566). Still far below ~800k extract+VEV-only bar; next iterations may ablate signed tilt vs this gate or tune 0.88/100.
